response.justification: Candidate has strong general software engineering experience across web, backend, and some embedded/hardware-adjacent work, but lacks the core requirements for this role: native-level Japanese proficiency is not indicated, C for UNIX development is minimal (one short stint at NI in 2014-2015), and there is no demonstrated experience with LAN-switch security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), Linux kernel modification, or networking protocols (NETCONF, SNMP). The profile leans heavily toward web/full-stack work in TypeScript/Node.js/DLang rather than embedded networking firmware.
strengths: Long, varied software engineering career with TDD and craftsmanship focus
Some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on a developer tooling product
Comfortable with Linux environments, Docker, and CI/CD infrastructure
concerns: No evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (only English, Romanian, basic German)
No embedded networking, switch firmware, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP)
No Linux kernel source-modification experience; C work is minimal and over a decade old

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking / switch security experience
Insufficient recent C / Linux kernel work
